steps.title: Read the color results
steps.content: The paper turned purple only in pear juice treated with E. coli O157:H7. It stayed pink with other E. coli strains and other bacteria, so the aptamers bound to the target while avoiding non-targets in a real-world food.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported comparisons
steps.content: Choice A compares O157:H7 tests with tests for other focal pathogens, which the study did not do. Choice B invents an explanation for uncertainty based on strain similarity. Choice C wrongly says the aptamers tended to bind to different strains. The result instead shows target binding plus specificity in the food sample. The answer is D.
